title: The Unified Current Deep

features.name: the convergence shed
features.desc-on-touch: A small wooden structure half-submerged in the current where three tributary channels merge into one. Tools for measuring water and marking levels hang on its walls. The shed's door faces downstream, as if it has accepted that it will eventually float away with the unified current.

features.name: the confluence marker
features.desc-on-touch: Three stones stand in the water where the tributaries meet, each carved with a different mark—the memory each stream brought with it before they learned to speak as one. The current slowly rounds their edges.

features.name: the deepening pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where the three currents meet, the water does not simply flow on—it spirals deeper, as if the unified current has learned something from the joining and must descend to think about it. The pool is perfectly circular, and nothing you drop into it ever resurfaces.

features.name: the record-keeper's shed
features.desc-on-touch: A second structure, newer, holds journals documenting the memory each tributary carried before convergence. The unified current has not erased these memories—it has simply changed their audience from singular to plural.

inscription: the tributaries remember themselves only here, in this moment before they become the larger current

file.name: Three_Rivers_Convergence_Field_Study_2004.mp3
file.note: A field recording of water sounds at the confluence point, processed to isolate each tributary's unique acoustic signature. The three channels were cleanly separated in the stereo field until the fina

exits.label: back upstream to where the gathering still remembers its parts
downstream into the deepening dark where current becomes current
along the new-unified channel where memory flows as one
